title: daboecia cantabrica f. alba
intro: Daboecia cantabrica f. alba, also known as the white mountain-heath, is a flowering plant in the heath family. It is a variant of the Daboecia cantabrica, a white-flowered heather plant native to the mountains of northern Spain and Portugal. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been found to have various health benefits, including reducing fever and soothing sore throats. Daboecia cantabrica f. alba can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures for medicinal use, although its preparation and usage should be guided by a healthcare professional.
